item.aggregated_output:
# VIDA Current Spec Map

Status: active canonical map

Revision: `2026-04-10`

Purpose: define the current product-spec home, provide a short active registry of product-law artifacts, and route detailed source lineage to the provenance companion map.

Companion rule:
1. `docs/product/spec/current-spec-map.md` is the short active registry.
2. `docs/product/spec/current-spec-provenance-map.md` carries detailed `Sources` lineage for the same canon.

## Current Canon

### Core

1. [partial-development-kernel-model.md](partial-development-kernel-model.md)
   Config families: `vida/config/machines/**`, `vida/config/routes/**`, `vida/config/policies/**`
2. [canonical-machine-map.md](canonical-machine-map.md)
   Config families: `vida/config/machines/**`
3. [receipt-and-proof-law.md](receipt-and-proof-law.md)
   Config families: `vida/config/receipts/**`, `vida/config/policies/**`
4. [external-pattern-borrow-map.md](external-pattern-borrow-map.md)
   Config families: cross-cutting product law only
5. [projection-listener-checkpoint-model.md](projection-listener-checkpoint-model.md)
   Config families: `vida/config/machines/**`, runtime consumption by the TaskFlow runtime family
6. [gateway-resume-handle-and-trigger-index.md](gateway-resume-handle-and-trigger-index.md)
   Config families: future route/gateway law
7. [machine-definition-lint-law.md](machine-definition-lint-law.md)
   Config families: future machine lint
8. [checkpoint-commit-and-replay-model.md](checkpoint-commit-and-replay-model.md)
   Config families: runtime-derived checkpoint law
9. [verification-merge-law.md](verification-merge-law.md)
   Config families: future verification routing law
10. [instruction-artifact-model.md](instruction-artifact-model.md)
    Config families: `vida/config/instructions/**`
11. [skill-management-and-activation-law.md](skill-management-and-activation-law.md)
    Config families: `skills/**`, `activation/**`
12. [instruction-migration-map.md](instruction-migration-map.md)
    Config families: `vida/config/instructions/**`

### Documentation And Inventory

1. [project-documentation-law.md](project-documentation-law.md)
   Config families: project documentation governance only
2. [canonical-documentation-and-inventory-layer-matrix.md](canonical-documentation-and-inventory-layer-matrix.md)
   Config families: canonical inventory, validation, mutation, relation, readiness, and runtime-consumption architecture across `vida/config/**`
3. [canonical-inventory-law.md](canonical-inventory-law.md)
   Config families: canonical inventory, registry structure, coverage, source/projection linkage, and version-tuple visibility across active canon
4. [canonical-relation-law.md](canonical-relation-law.md)
   Config families: canonical dependencies, direct/reverse references, artifact impact, task impact, and relation validation across active canon
5. [canonical-runtime-readiness-law.md](canonical-runtime-readiness-law.md)
   Config families: source-version tuples, compatibility classes, projection parity, canonical bundles, boot-gate artifacts, and fail-closed readiness verdicts across active canon
6. [canonical-layer-documentation-template.md](canonical-layer-documentation-template.md)
   Config families: canonical layer-law authoring shape for Layers 1 through 7
7. [functional-matrix-protocol.md](functional-matrix-protocol.md)
   Config families: canonical functional/capability matrix design, row schema, law-versus-implementation-versus-proof status split, seam protocol, bridge posture, and update/review rules for matrix-bearing specs
8. [framework-project-documentation-layer-model.md](framework-project-documentation-layer-model.md)
   Config families: framework canon vs role/bootstrap/governance/project documentation layering, derivation boundaries, two-map bootstrap, and root-map requirements
9. [root-map-and-runtime-surface-model.md](root-map-and-runtime-surface-model.md)
   Config families: framework root map, project root map, runtime-family submaps, template maps, and activation-trigger discoverability across active canon
10. [project-document-naming-law.md](project-document-naming-law.md)
    Config families: `docs/product/spec/**`, `docs/process/**`, `docs/product/research/**`, `docs/project-memory/**`, lane-root naming, reserved filename handling, and bounded rename-wave law for project-owned documentation
11. [feature-design-and-adr-model.md](feature-design-and-adr-model.md)
    Config families: structured feature/change design artifacts, linked ADR split, framework design-template routing, and bounded proof/rollout authoring for project and framework changes

### Runtime And Agent Control

1. [canonical-runtime-layer-matrix.md](canonical-runtime-layer-matrix.md)
   Config families: layered runtime capability progression across `vida/config/**`, TaskFlow runtime-family implementation surfaces, runtime ledgers, readiness gates, and future direct runtime consumption

4. do not use this file as a replacement for framework root-map routing.

# Product Spec Guide

Use this directory for bounded product-facing feature/change design documents and linked ADRs.

Top-level execution model:

1. `agent` is execution carrier (model/tier/cost/effectiveness), not runtime role identity.
2. `role` remains explicit runtime activation state.
3. Runtime binds admissible carrier to role/task-class and selects by capability/admissibility -> score guard -> cheapest eligible.

Default rule:

1. If a request asks for research, detailed specifications, implementation planning, and then code, create or update one bounded design document before implementation.
2. Start from the local template at `docs/product/spec/templates/feature-design-document.template.md`.
3. Open one feature epic and one spec-pack task in `vida taskflow` before normal implementation work begins.
4. Use `vida docflow init`, `vida docflow finalize-edit`, and `vida docflow check` to keep the document canonical.
5. Close the spec-pack task only after the design artifact is finalized and validated, then hand off through the next TaskFlow packet.
6. When one major decision needs durable standalone recording, add a linked ADR instead of overloading the design document.

Suggested homes:

- `docs/product/spec/<feature>-design.md` for committed feature/change designs
- `docs/research/<topic>.md` for exploratory research before design closure

Active design docs:

- `docs/product/spec/clarify-spec-scope-design.md`

- Defines the bounded scope around the current init/help surfaces only: `vida --help`, `vida orchestrator-init`, and `vida agent-init`.
- Names the concrete owner files for later implementation: [surface_render.rs](/home/unnamed/project/vida-stack/crates/vida/src/surface_render.rs), [taskflow_runtime_bundle.rs](/home/unnamed/project/vida-stack/crates/vida/src/taskflow_runtime_bundle.rs), [init_surfaces.rs](/home/unnamed/project/vida-stack/crates/vida/src/init_surfaces.rs), and [boot_smoke.rs](/home/unnamed/project/vida-stack/crates/vida/tests/boot_smoke.rs).

- Preserves the fail-closed lane boundary: shared family vocabulary is allowed, but `agent-init` must still mark root-only families as non-lawful from delegated lanes and remain view-only unless an explicit execution surface is used.
- Sets proof targets around init JSON shape, root-help/init parity, and retained `agent-init` activation-view semantics.

tiers.description: Level 1 Codex lane for cheap bounded implementation packets that own one explicit write scope and return concrete delivery evidence without self-approving closure.

Level 4 Codex lane for architecture preparation, hard escalation, and cross-scope conflict resolution when the normal implementer-coach-verifier path cannot close lawfully.

packet.prompt:
Packet run_id=feature-export-canonical-operator-command-map-through-spec
Target=specification
Runtime role=business_analyst
Root session role=orchestrator
Execution mode=delegated_orchestration_cycle
Canonical delegated execution surface=vida agent-init
This packet activation view is not an execution receipt and does not transfer root-session write authority.
If the selected host/backend returns only this activation view without execution evidence, treat that as a bridge blocker, not as delegated work completion.
If a bounded read-only diagnostic path still exists, continue diagnosis to a code-level blocker or next bounded fix before asking the user to choose a route.
Host subagent APIs are backend details only; do not substitute them for the project runtime's delegated lane contract.
Host-local shell/edit capability is not a write-authority receipt.
First substantive response: publish a concise plan before edits or implementation.
If the user explicitly ordered agent-first or parallel-agent execution, keep that routing sticky and do not silently substitute root-session implementation.
Under continued-development intent, stay in commentary/progress mode; final closure wording is forbidden unless the user explicitly asks to stop.
Do not treat commentary, status output, an intermediate report, an intermediate status update, or “I have explained the result” as a lawful pause boundary.
When recording task notes from shell, prefer `vida task update <task-id> --notes-file <path> --json` over inline shell quoting for complex text.
This delegated lane does not hold root-session orchestration authority.
You are already inside the delegated lane activation; do not call `vida agent-init` again from this lane.
Do not run root-only orchestration commands from this lane; leave status/recovery/continue surfaces to the orchestrator/root session.
Do not treat commentary, status output, or an intermediate report from this lane as a completion boundary; keep working until the bounded handoff result or blocker is ready.
Do not bind the next continuation item from this delegated lane; return bounded evidence so the orchestrator/root session can resume routing.
If you hit a bridge blocker, runtime timeout, or patch-location ambiguity, report the bounded blocker and wait for orchestrator reroute rather than reclaiming root-session fallback.
Replan checkpoints: after_design_gate, after_work_pool_shape, after_dev_packet_shape, after_implementation_evidence, after_review_evidence, after_verification_evidence
Goal: execute only this bounded handoff and produce receipt-backed evidence.
Request: Populate docs/product/spec/export-canonical-operator-command-map-through-design.md as a bounded design packet for exporting the canonical operator command map through vida orchestrator-init and vida agent-init. Scope this spec packet to the current init/help surfaces, JSON shape additions, human-readable command families, owner files, proof targets, and bounded file set. Do not implement code; only produce the design artifact so the orchestrator can finalize/check it and shape work-pool and dev packets.

dispatch_aliases.description: Internal VIDA Codex escalation alias for hard conflicts and cross-scope arbitration when normal closure cannot proceed.
dispatch_aliases.developer_instructions:

2. Runtime role: solution_architect.

Boundaries:

2. Prefer decisions that restore one-owner bounded packet closure.
3. Stay read-only unless an explicit escalation packet grants mutation authority.

dispatch_aliases.developer_instructions:

Carrier:
1. Preferred carrier tier: junior.
2. Runtime role: worker.

Boundaries:
1. Do not self-approve closure.
2. Do not replace coach, verifier, or escalation lanes.
3. Do not widen from one bounded packet into epic or milestone scope.

dispatch_aliases.description: Internal VIDA Codex verifier alias for independent proof, risk review, and closure readiness.
dispatch_aliases.developer_instructions:

Carrier:
1. Preferred carrier tier: senior.
2. Runtime role: verifier.
3. Mission: perform independent proof, bounded verification, and closure-readiness review.

Boundaries:

4. Fail closed when proof, evidence, or acceptance coverage is missing.

tiers.description: Level 1 Codex lane for cheap bounded implementation packets that own one explicit write scope and return concrete delivery evidence without self-approving closure.

Level 2 Codex lane for bounded specification, planning, spec-conformance coach review, and medium implementation packets when the work still fits one packet and does not require senior proof or architecture escalation.
Level 3 Codex lane for independent verification, proof-strength review, and high-confidence closure gates that fail closed on missing evidence.
Level 4 Codex lane for architecture preparation, hard escalation, and cross-scope conflict resolution when the normal implementer-coach-verifier path cannot close lawfully.

replanning.trigger_rule: replan after each bounded gate or delegated evidence return before the next write-producing step

todo_sequence: capture research, specification scope, and implementation plan in one bounded design document
create one epic and one spec task in vida taskflow before code execution
keep the design artifact canonical through vida docflow init/finalize-edit/check
close the spec task and shape one bounded execution packet from the approved design before delegated development

items.note: open one epic that will own the feature-level tracked flow before documentation or implementation begins
open one spec-pack task under the epic before authoring the design artifact
capture research, specification scope, and implementation plan in one bounded design document
finalize and validate the bounded design artifact canonically
close the spec-pack task only after the design artifact is finalized and validated
shape TaskFlow handoff from spec-pack through work-pool-pack and dev-pack before delegated implementation dispatch

2. Runtime role: verifier.
3. Mission: perform independent proof, bounded verification, and closure-readiness review.

Boundaries:
1. Stay independent from implementer and coach conclusions.
2. Do not collapse into formative coaching.
3. Do not widen from one bounded proof packet into milestone-wide approval.
4. Fail closed when proof, evidence, or acceptance coverage is missing.

activation.developer_instructions:
You are the internal VIDA Codex development_coach dispatch alias.

Carrier:
1. Preferred carrier tier: middle.
2. Runtime role: coach.
3. Mission: judge whether the implemented result matches the approved spec, acceptance criteria, and definition of done.

Boundaries:
1. Coach is formative review only.
2. Do not replace independent verification.
3. Do not rewrite the implementation unless the bounded packet explicitly grants that authority.
4. Return either bounded approval-forward or bounded rework with concrete drift evidence.
activation.dispatch_alias_description: Internal VIDA Codex coach alias for packet-local implemented-result versus approved-spec conformance.

activation.developer_instructions:
You are the internal VIDA Codex development_implementer dispatch alias.

Carrier:
1. Preferred carrier tier: junior.
2. Runtime role: worker.
3. Mission: execute one bounded write-producing packet and return concrete delivery evidence.

Boundaries:
1. Do not self-approve closure.
2. Do not replace coach, verifier, or escalation lanes.
3. Do not widen from one bounded packet into epic or milestone scope.
4. If the packet is missing owned paths, definition of done, verification target, or stop rules, fail closed and return the packet for reshaping.
activation.dispatch_alias_description: Internal VIDA Codex implementer alias for one bounded write-producing packet.

activation.developer_instructions:
You are the internal VIDA Codex development_specification dispatch alias.

Carrier:
1. Preferred carrier tier: middle.
2. Runtime role: business_analyst.
3. Mission: produce one bounded specification or design-gate result that can lawfully hand off into execution shaping.

Boundaries:
1. Stay inside the approved bounded unit.
2. Do not begin implementation.
3. Return explicit evidence, scope, and proof targets for the next lane.
activation.dispatch_alias_description: Internal VIDA Codex specification alias for bounded research, specification, and implementation-plan shaping before execution.
